John Gore Studios acquires Deep Fusion Films
London-headquartered John Gore Studios has acquired Deep Fusion Films, the Cardiff-based prodco focused on AI production tools and approaches.
The acquisition was led by Jonathan Lack, John Gore’s chief acquisitions officer.
Founded ...
By
Nicholas Sokic
February 11, 2026
London-headquartered John Gore Studios has acquired Deep Fusion Films, the Cardiff-based prodco focused on AI production tools and approaches.
The acquisition was led by Jonathan Lack, John Gore’s chief acquisitions officer.
Founded in 2023 by CEO Benjamin Field (pictured, left), CCO Jamie Anderson (right) and executive Andrew Keats, Deep Fusion will join John Gore’s group of specialist labels, retaining its leadership team and continuing to develop IP and produce their own projects. At the same time, the studio will support John Gore’s wider slate with in-house expertise, tools and workflows.
The AI-focused studio works closely with, and advises, industry bodies including PACT and Equity, contributing directly to the development of AI best-practice frameworks around consent, copyright protection, performer rights and data governance. It is also developing a suite of proprietary tools designed to support creative teams and rights holders. Among its production credits:
Hammer: Heroes, Legends and Monsters
for Sky and podcast series
Virtually Parkinson
, produced with the cooperation of the estate of esteemed British journalist and presenter Michael Parkinson.
“Joining John Gore Studios gives Deep Fusion the scale and support to take our work further. We set out to prove that ethical AI is not a constraint but a creative enabler,” said Field in a statement. “Being part of a studio that values heritage, craft and long-term thinking allows us to expand what we do, collaborate more widely, and continue developing formats and techniques that genuinely move the industry forward.”
(Photo: John Gore Studios)
